papers.title: MiA-Signature: Approximating Global Activation for Long-Context Understanding
papers.abstract: Abstract Researchers propose a compressed representation method for global activation patterns in large language models that approximates full activation states while maintaining computational efficiency and improving performance in long-context tasks.  AI-generated summary A growing body of work in cognitive science suggests that reportable conscious access is associated with global ignition over distributed memory systems, while such activation is only partially accessible as individuals cannot directly access or enumerate all activated contents. This tension suggests a plausible mechanism that cognition may rely on a compact representation that approximates the global influence of activation on downstream processing. Inspired by this idea, we introduce the concept of Mindscape Activation Signature (MiA-Signature), a compressed representation of the global activation pattern induced by a query. In LLM systems, this is instantiated via submodular-based selection of high-level concepts that cover the activated context space, optionally refined through lightweight iterative updates using working memory. The resulting MiA-Signature serves as a conditioning signal that approximates the effect of the full activation state while remaining computationally tractable. Integrating MiA-Signatures into both RAG and agentic systems yields consistent performance gains across multiple long-context understanding tasks.
affiliations: Institute of Information Engineering, Chinese Academy of Sciences
Pattern Recognition Center, WeChat AI, Tencent
School of Cyber Security, University of Chinese Academy of Sciences

ru.title: Компактная сигнатура активации для эффективной обработки длинных контекстов в LLM
ru.desc: Исследователи предложили метод сжатого представления глобальных паттернов активации в больших языковых моделях, вдохновленный когнитивной наукой. Они вводят концепцию MiA-Signature — компактное представление активационного паттерна, которое аппроксимирует полное состояние активации, используя субмодулярный отбор ключевых концепций. Метод сохраняет вычислительную эффективность и может быть дополнен легкими итеративными обновлениями через рабочую память. Интеграция MiA-Signatures в системы RAG и agentные системы показывает улучшение производительности на задачах понимания длинных контекстов.

en.title: Efficient Activation Representation for Enhanced Long-Context Understanding
en.desc: This paper introduces a new method called Mindscape Activation Signature (MiA-Signature) for efficiently representing the activation patterns in large language models (LLMs). The MiA-Signature captures the essential global activation information while reducing computational costs, making it suitable for tasks that involve long contexts. By using a submodular selection of high-level concepts, the method approximates the full activation state, allowing for better performance in understanding complex inputs. The integration of MiA-Signatures into retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) and agentic systems shows significant improvements in task performance.

zh.title: 压缩全局激活模式，提升长上下文任务性能
zh.desc: 研究人员提出了一种压缩表示方法，用于大型语言模型中的全局激活模式。这种方法在保持计算效率的同时，能够近似完整的激活状态，并在长上下文任务中提高性能。我们引入了心智激活特征（MiA-Signature）的概念，它通过选择高层次概念来覆盖激活的上下文空间，从而形成压缩的全局激活模式表示。将MiA-Signature集成到RAG和智能系统中，可以在多个长上下文理解任务中实现一致的性能提升。

papers.title: MARBLE: Multi-Aspect Reward Balance for Diffusion RL
papers.abstract: Abstract A novel gradient-space optimization framework called MARBLE addresses limitations in multi-reward reinforcement learning fine-tuning of diffusion models by maintaining independent advantage estimators and harmonizing policy gradients through quadratic programming without manual reward weighting.  AI-generated summary Reinforcement learning fine-tuning has become the dominant approach for aligning diffusion models with human preferences. However, assessing images is intrinsically a multi-dimensional task, and multiple evaluation criteria need to be optimized simultaneously. Existing practice deal with multiple rewards by training one specialist model per reward, optimizing a weighted-sum reward R(x)=sum_k w_k R_k(x), or sequentially fine-tuning with a hand-crafted stage schedule. These approaches either fail to produce a unified model that can be jointly trained on all rewards or necessitates heavy manually tuned sequential training. We find that the failure stems from using a naive weighted-sum reward aggregation. This approach suffers from a sample-level mismatch because most rollouts are specialist samples, highly informative for certain reward dimensions but irrelevant for others; consequently, weighted summation dilutes their supervision. To address this issue, we propose MARBLE (Multi-Aspect Reward BaLancE), a gradient-space optimization framework that maintains independent advantage estimators for each reward, computes per-reward policy gradients, and harmonizes them into a single update direction without manually-tuned reward weighting, by solving a Quadratic Programming problem. We further propose an amortized formulation that exploits the affine structure of the loss used in DiffusionNFT, to reduce the per-step cost from K+1 backward passes to near single-reward baseline cost, together with EMA smoothing on the balancing coefficients to stabilize updates against transient single-batch fluctuations. On SD3.5 Medium with five rewards, MARBLE improves all five reward dimensions simultaneously, turns the worst-aligned reward's gradient cosine from negative under weighted summation in 80% of mini-batches to consistently positive, and runs at 0.97X the training speed of baseline training.

ru.title: Гармония градиентов: многокритериальная оптимизация без ручной балансировки
ru.desc: Статья представляет MARBLE — новый фреймворк оптимизации в пространстве градиентов для многокритериального обучения с подкреплением при тонкой настройке диффузионных моделей. Вместо традиционного взвешенного суммирования вознаграждений, метод поддерживает независимые оценки преимущества для каждого критерия и гармонизирует политические градиенты через квадратичное программирование без ручного подбора весов. Подход решает проблему несоответствия образцов на уровне отдельных примеров, когда специализированные примеры высокоинформативны только для одного критерия. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что MARBLE одновременно улучшает все пять критериев на SD3.5 Medium с минимальными вычислительными затратами.

en.title: Harmonizing Multi-Reward Learning with MARBLE
en.desc: The paper introduces MARBLE, a new framework for optimizing multi-reward reinforcement learning in diffusion models. It addresses the challenges of using weighted-sum reward aggregation, which can dilute the effectiveness of training by mixing specialist samples that are only relevant for certain rewards. MARBLE maintains independent advantage estimators for each reward and harmonizes policy gradients through quadratic programming, eliminating the need for manual reward weighting. This approach not only improves the alignment of multiple rewards but also enhances training efficiency, achieving better performance across all reward dimensions simultaneously.

zh.title: MARBLE：多奖励优化的新方法
zh.desc: MARBLE是一种新的梯度空间优化框架，旨在解决多奖励强化学习在微调扩散模型时的局限性。它通过保持独立的优势估计器，并利用二次规划和谐化策略梯度，避免了手动奖励加权的问题。与传统方法不同，MARBLE能够同时优化多个评估标准，而不需要为每个奖励训练单独的模型。实验结果表明，MARBLE在多个奖励维度上均有显著提升，并且训练速度接近基线的97%。

papers.title: Continuous Latent Diffusion Language Model
papers.abstract: Abstract Cola DLM presents a hierarchical latent diffusion language model that uses text-to-latent mapping, global semantic prior modeling, and conditional decoding to achieve efficient text generation with flexible non-autoregressive inductive bias.  AI-generated summary Large language models have achieved remarkable success under the autoregressive paradigm, yet high-quality text generation need not be tied to a fixed left-to-right order. Existing alternatives still struggle to jointly achieve generation efficiency, scalable representation learning, and effective global semantic modeling. We propose Cola DLM, a hierarchical latent diffusion language model that frames text generation through hierarchical information decomposition. Cola DLM first learns a stable text-to-latent mapping with a Text VAE, then models a global semantic prior in continuous latent space with a block-causal DiT, and finally generates text through conditional decoding. From a unified Markov-path perspective, its diffusion process performs latent prior transport rather than token-level observation recovery, thereby separating global semantic organization from local textual realization. This design yields a more flexible non-autoregressive inductive bias, supports semantic compression and prior fitting in continuous space, and naturally extends to other continuous modalities. Through experiments spanning 4 research questions, 8 benchmarks, strictly matched ~2B-parameter autoregressive and LLaDA baselines, and scaling curves up to about 2000 EFLOPs, we identify an effective overall configuration of Cola DLM and verify its strong scaling behavior for text generation. Taken together, the results establish hierarchical continuous latent prior modeling as a principled alternative to strictly token-level language modeling, where generation quality and scaling behavior may better reflect model capability than likelihood, while also suggesting a concrete path toward unified modeling across discrete text and continuous modalities.

ru.title: Семантический приор в скрытом пространстве вместо токена за токеном
ru.desc: Cola DLM — это иерархическая модель диффузии в скрытом пространстве для генерации текста, которая разделяет процесс на три этапа: обучение стабильного отображения текста в латентное пространство через Text VAE, моделирование глобального семантического приора с помощью блочно-причинного трансформатора, и условное декодирование текста. В отличие от традиционных авторегрессивных моделей, Cola DLM использует неавторегрессивную индуктивное смещение и переносит приор в непрерывном скрытом пространстве вместо восстановления токенов на уровне наблюдений. Такой подход обеспечивает более эффективную генерацию текста, лучшую масштабируемость представления и более гибкую моделирование глобальной семантики. Экспериментальные результаты на 8 бенчмарках показывают, что иерархическое моделирование семантического приора в непрерывном пространстве является перспективной альтернативой традиционному токен-уровневому языковому моделированию.

en.title: Revolutionizing Text Generation with Hierarchical Latent Diffusion
en.desc: The paper introduces Cola DLM, a hierarchical latent diffusion language model designed for efficient text generation. It utilizes a text-to-latent mapping and models global semantics in a continuous latent space, allowing for flexible non-autoregressive generation. By separating global semantic organization from local text realization, Cola DLM enhances generation efficiency and representation learning. The experiments demonstrate its strong performance compared to traditional autoregressive models, suggesting a new approach to language modeling that integrates discrete and continuous modalities.

zh.title: 层次化潜在扩散模型：文本生成的新路径
zh.desc: Cola DLM是一种层次化的潜在扩散语言模型，采用文本到潜在空间的映射、全局语义先验建模和条件解码来实现高效的文本生成。与传统的自回归模型不同，Cola DLM通过层次信息分解来处理文本生成，提供了更灵活的非自回归归纳偏置。该模型首先使用文本变分自编码器（Text VAE）学习稳定的文本到潜在空间的映射，然后在连续潜在空间中建模全局语义先验，最后通过条件解码生成文本。实验结果表明，Cola DLM在文本生成方面具有强大的扩展性和生成质量，展示了层次化连续潜在先验建模作为一种有效的替代方案。

papers.title: When to Trust Imagination: Adaptive Action Execution for World Action Models
papers.abstract: Abstract  World Action Models (WAMs) have recently emerged as a promising paradigm for robotic manipulation by jointly predicting future visual observations and future actions. However, current WAMs typically execute a fixed number of predicted actions after each model inference, leaving the robot blind to whether the imagined future remains consistent with the actual physical rollout. In this work, we formulate adaptive WAM execution as a future-reality verification problem: the robot should execute longer when the WAM-predicted future remains reliable, and replan earlier when reality deviates from imagination. To this end, we propose Future Forward Dynamics Causal Attention (FFDC), a lightweight verifier that jointly reasons over predicted future actions, predicted visual dynamics, real observations, and language instructions to estimate whether the remaining action rollout can still be trusted. FFDC enables adaptive action chunk sizes as an emergent consequence of prediction-observation consistency, preserving the efficiency of long-horizon execution while restoring responsiveness in contact-rich or difficult phases. We further introduce Mixture-of-Horizon Training to improve long-horizon trajectory coverage for adaptive execution. Experiments on the RoboTwin benchmark and in the real world demonstrate that our method achieves a strong robustness-efficiency trade-off: on RoboTwin, it reduces WAM forward passes by 69.10% and execution time by 34.02%, while improving success rate by 2.54% over the short-chunk baseline; in real-world experiments, it improves success rate by 35%.
affiliations: Southern University of Science and Technology, Shenzhen, China
The University of Hong Kong, Hong Kong, China

ru.title: Адаптивное выполнение действий робота через проверку согласованности предсказаний и реальности
ru.desc: В работе предлагается адаптивный подход к выполнению действий роботом, основанный на проверке согласованности между предсказаниями модели мира и реальными наблюдениями. Авторы вводят FFDC (Future Forward Dynamics Causal Attention) — легковесный верификатор, который анализирует предсказанные действия, визуальную динамику и реальные наблюдения для оценки надежности оставшихся действий. Метод позволяет адаптивно изменять размер блоков действий в зависимости от предсказания, сохраняя эффективность долгоплановой навигации и повышая отзывчивость в сложных фазах манипуляции. Экспериментальные результаты показывают значительное улучшение баланса между надежностью и эффективностью выполнения задач роботом.

en.title: Adaptive Action Execution for Reliable Robotic Manipulation
en.desc: This paper introduces a new approach to World Action Models (WAMs) for robotic manipulation, focusing on adaptive execution based on future-reality verification. The proposed Future Forward Dynamics Causal Attention (FFDC) system allows robots to adjust their actions dynamically, ensuring they remain aligned with real-world observations. By evaluating the consistency between predicted actions and actual outcomes, FFDC enhances the robot's ability to respond effectively during complex tasks. The method shows significant improvements in efficiency and success rates in both simulated and real-world environments, demonstrating its practical applicability.

zh.title: 自适应动作执行：让机器人更聪明的未来验证
zh.desc: 世界动作模型（WAMs）是一种新兴的机器人操作方法，通过预测未来的视觉观察和动作来实现。然而，现有的WAMs在每次模型推理后通常执行固定数量的预测动作，这使得机器人无法判断想象的未来是否与实际情况一致。本文将自适应WAM执行形式化为未来现实验证问题：当WAM预测的未来可靠时，机器人应执行更长的动作；当现实与想象偏离时，应提前重新规划。我们提出了一种轻量级验证器FFDC，能够联合推理预测的未来动作、视觉动态、真实观察和语言指令，以评估剩余动作的可信度。

papers.title: Continuous-Time Distribution Matching for Few-Step Diffusion Distillation
papers.abstract: Abstract Continuous-Time Distribution Matching migrates diffusion model distillation from discrete to continuous optimization, enabling arbitrary points along sampling trajectories and preserving fine visual details through dynamic scheduling and velocity field extrapolation.  AI-generated summary Step distillation has become a leading technique for accelerating diffusion models, among which Distribution Matching Distillation (DMD) and Consistency Distillation are two representative paradigms. While consistency methods enforce self-consistency along the full PF-ODE trajectory to steer it toward the clean data manifold, vanilla DMD relies on sparse supervision at a few predefined discrete timesteps. This restricted discrete-time formulation and mode-seeking nature of the reverse KL divergence tends to exhibit visual artifacts and over-smoothed outputs, often necessitating complex auxiliary modules -- such as GANs or reward models -- to restore visual fidelity. In this work, we introduce Continuous-Time Distribution Matching (CDM), migrating the DMD framework from discrete anchoring to continuous optimization for the first time. CDM achieves this through two continuous-time designs. First, we replace the fixed discrete schedule with a dynamic continuous schedule of random length, so that distribution matching is enforced at arbitrary points along sampling trajectories rather than only at a few fixed anchors. Second, we propose a continuous-time alignment objective that performs active off-trajectory matching on latents extrapolated via the student's velocity field, improving generalization and preserving fine visual details. Extensive experiments on different architectures, including SD3-Medium and Longcat-Image, demonstrate that CDM provides highly competitive visual fidelity for few-step image generation without relying on complex auxiliary objectives. Code is available at https://github.com/byliutao/cdm.
affiliations: College of Artificial Intelligence, Jilin University
VCIP, College of Computer Science, Nankai University

ru.title: От дискретных анкеров к непрерывной оптимизации в дистилляции диффузионных моделей
ru.desc: Статья представляет Continuous-Time Distribution Matching (CDM) — новый метод дистилляции диффузионных моделей, который переходит от дискретной оптимизации к непрерывной. Вместо привязки к фиксированным временным шагам, метод выполняет сопоставление распределений в произвольных точках траектории генерации. Авторы предлагают динамическое расписание случайной длины и объектив выравнивания, основанный на экстраполяции поля скоростей студента, что позволяет сохранить мелкие визуальные детали. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что CDM обеспечивает высокое качество генерации изображений за несколько шагов без использования вспомогательных модулей.

en.title: Revolutionizing Diffusion Models with Continuous Optimization
en.desc: This paper presents Continuous-Time Distribution Matching (CDM), a new approach that enhances diffusion model distillation by shifting from discrete to continuous optimization. CDM allows for sampling at any point along the trajectory, which helps maintain fine visual details and reduces artifacts. The method introduces a dynamic scheduling system and a continuous-time alignment objective, improving the model's ability to generalize and match distributions effectively. Experiments show that CDM achieves high visual fidelity in image generation with fewer steps, without needing complex additional components.

zh.title: 连续时间分布匹配：提升扩散模型的视觉质量
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种新的连续时间分布匹配方法（CDM），将扩散模型的蒸馏从离散优化迁移到连续优化。CDM通过动态调度和速度场外推，能够在采样轨迹的任意点进行分布匹配，从而保留细致的视觉细节。与传统的离散时间方法相比，CDM避免了视觉伪影和过度平滑的问题，减少了对复杂辅助模块的依赖。实验结果表明，CDM在少步图像生成中提供了高度竞争的视觉保真度。

papers.title: Nonsense Helps: Prompt Space Perturbation Broadens Reasoning Exploration
papers.abstract: Abstract LoPE addresses the zero-advantage problem in reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards by usingLorem Ipsum perturbations to enhance exploration in large language model training.  AI-generated summary Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards, particularly Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO), has significantly advanced the reasoning capabilities of Large Language Models (LLMs). However, in complex tasks, GRPO frequently suffers from the ``zero-advantage problem'': when all sampled rollouts for a query fail, the relative advantage collapses to zero. Consequently, the model loses effective training signals for these questions, wasting the training data and computational budget. While simply increasing the sampling budget for these questions is a common remedy, the static sampling policy inherently constrains reasoning exploration, limiting the success rate. In this paper, we propose Lorem Perturbation for Exploration (LoPE), a simple yet effective training framework to break this exploration bottleneck. We posit that task-irrelevant prompt-space perturbations can shift the model's output distribution enough to unlock orthogonal reasoning pathways for hard questions. Specifically, LoPE prepends sequences stochastically assembled from Lorem Ipsum vocabulary (a pseudo-Latin placeholder text) to the prompts before resampling. Experiments across 1.7B, 4B, and 7B models demonstrate that LoPE significantly outperforms resampling with the original prompts. Further analysis reveals that other Latin-based random sequences with low perplexity are also effective perturbations. Our results establish LoPE as a strong baseline for broadening exploration in LLM reinforcement learning.

ru.title: Разнообразие через шум: как случайные возмущения помогают моделям находить новые пути решения
ru.desc: Статья рассматривает проблему нулевого преимущества в обучении с подкреплением больших языковых моделей, когда все сгенерированные ответы на сложный вопрос оказываются неправильными и модель теряет полезный сигнал для обучения. Авторы предлагают LoPE — метод, который добавляет возмущения на основе Lorem Ipsum текста к исходным промптам, чтобы разнообразить стратегию генерации и открыть альтернативные пути рассуждения. Эксперименты на моделях размером от 1.7B до 7B параметров показывают, что такие задачи-несвязанные возмущения в пространстве промптов значительно повышают успешность поиска правильных ответов по сравнению с простым увеличением количества попыток. Исследование демонстрирует, что другие низкопроизводные латинские последовательности также эффективны, что подтверждает универсальность подхода к расширению исследовательского поведения при обучении LLM с подкреплением.

en.title: Unlocking Exploration in Reinforcement Learning with LoPE
en.desc: This paper introduces Lorem Perturbation for Exploration (LoPE), a method designed to tackle the zero-advantage problem in reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards. The zero-advantage problem occurs when all sampled rollouts fail, leading to a loss of effective training signals. LoPE enhances exploration by adding random sequences from Lorem Ipsum to prompts, which helps the model discover new reasoning pathways. Experiments show that this approach significantly improves performance compared to traditional resampling methods.

zh.title: 打破探索瓶颈，提升推理能力
zh.desc: LoPE是一种针对强化学习中零优势问题的解决方案，特别是在可验证奖励的情况下。该方法通过使用Lorem Ipsum扰动来增强大语言模型的探索能力，从而改善模型在复杂任务中的推理能力。传统的采样策略在面对困难问题时容易导致训练信号丢失，而LoPE通过随机添加与任务无关的文本扰动，帮助模型找到新的推理路径。实验结果表明，LoPE在不同规模的模型中均显著优于传统的重采样方法。

papers.title: Auto Research with Specialist Agents Develops Effective and Non-Trivial Training Recipes
papers.abstract: Abstract Auto research operates as an empirical loop where agents iteratively refine code based on evaluation feedback, achieving improved performance across multiple tasks without human intervention.  AI-generated summary We study auto research as a closed empirical loop driven by external measurement. Each submitted trial carries a hypothesis, an executable code edit, an evaluator-owned outcome, and feedback that shapes the next proposal. The output is not a generated paper or a single model checkpoint, but an auditable trajectory of proposals, code diffs, experiments, scores, and failure labels. We instantiate this loop with specialist agents that partition recipe surfaces and share measured lineage across trials. The central empirical finding is that lineage feedback lets agents turn evaluator outcomes, including crashes, budget overruns, size failures, and accuracy-gate misses, into later program-level recipe edits rather than one-shot suggestions. Across 1,197 headline-run trials plus 600 Parameter Golf control trials after one-time setup and launch, humans did not choose proposals, edit recipes, override scores, or repair failed trials during the search. In the three headline runs, the same submitted-trial loop reduces Parameter Golf validation bpb by 0.81%, raises NanoChat-D12 CORE by 38.7%, and reduces CIFAR-10 Airbench96 wallclock by 4.59%, with each task measured by its own external evaluator and legality checks. The trace includes a strict architecture-domain audit of 157 headline-run submissions and program rewrites such as a NanoChat attention-kernel path change. Within this scope the loop autonomously writes code, submits experiments, absorbs feedback, applies and combines known techniques inside each environment, and improves public starting recipes.
affiliations: School of Computer Science, Carnegie Mellon University

ru.title: Автономные агенты учатся на ошибках через замкнутый цикл обратной связи
ru.desc: Статья описывает автоматизированный цикл исследований, в котором агенты итеративно улучшают код на основе обратной связи от оценивателей без участия человека. Система работает как замкнутый эмпирический цикл, где каждая попытка содержит гипотезу, изменения кода, результаты оценки и обратную связь для следующей итерации. Ключевое открытие заключается в том, что обратная связь от оценивателей (включая ошибки и превышения бюджета) преобразуется агентами в улучшения на уровне программы, а не просто в единовременные предложения. На практике система достигла улучшений в трёх задачах: снизила метрику валидации на 0.81%, повысила производительность модели на 38.7% и уменьшила время выполнения на 4.59%.

en.title: Autonomous Code Refinement Through Feedback Loops
en.desc: This paper explores the concept of auto research in machine learning, where agents autonomously refine their code based on feedback from evaluations. The process involves submitting trials that include hypotheses, code changes, and outcomes, which inform future proposals. The study demonstrates that feedback from previous trials can be used to make informed edits to the code, rather than relying on one-time suggestions. The results show significant improvements in various tasks without human intervention, highlighting the effectiveness of this iterative, empirical approach.

zh.title: 自动研究：无须人类干预的自我优化循环
zh.desc: 这篇论文研究了自动研究作为一个封闭的经验循环，利用外部测量来驱动改进。每次提交的试验都包含假设、可执行的代码编辑、评估者拥有的结果和反馈，这些反馈会影响下一次提案。研究发现，反馈可以帮助代理将评估结果转化为后续的程序级别编辑，而不是一次性的建议。通过大量的实验，系统在没有人类干预的情况下，自动生成代码、提交实验并吸收反馈，从而不断提高性能。

papers.title: TabEmbed: Benchmarking and Learning Generalist Embeddings for Tabular Understanding
papers.abstract: Abstract A new generalist embedding model called TabEmbed is introduced that unifies tabular classification and retrieval tasks within a shared embedding space using large-scale contrastive learning with positive-aware hard negative mining.  AI-generated summary Foundation models have established unified representations for natural language processing, yet this paradigm remains largely unexplored for tabular data. Existing methods face fundamental limitations: LLM-based approaches lack retrieval-compatible vector outputs, whereas text embedding models often fail to capture tabular structure and numerical semantics. To bridge this gap, we first introduce the Tabular Embedding Benchmark (TabBench), a comprehensive suite designed to evaluate the tabular understanding capability of embedding models. We then propose TabEmbed, the first generalist embedding model that unifies tabular classification and retrieval within a shared embedding space. By reformulating diverse tabular tasks as semantic matching problems, TabEmbed leverages large-scale contrastive learning with positive-aware hard negative mining to discern fine-grained structural and numerical nuances. Experimental results on TabBench demonstrate that TabEmbed significantly outperforms state-of-the-art text embedding models, establishing a new baseline for universal tabular representation learning. Code and datasets are publicly available at https://github.com/qiangminjie27/TabEmbed and https://huggingface.co/datasets/qiangminjie27/TabBench.
affiliations: Natural Language Processing Lab, Soochow University, Suzhou, China
The Hong Kong Polytechnic University, Hong Kong, China

ru.title: Единое представление для таблиц: embedding model для классификации и поиска
ru.desc: В статье представлена модель TabEmbed — первый обобщённый embedding model для табличных данных, который объединяет задачи классификации и поиска информации в едином пространстве представлений. Авторы используют контрастивное обучение в больших масштабах с методом добычи сложных отрицательных примеров, осведомленных о положительных образцах, чтобы модель лучше понимала структуру таблиц и семантику чисел. Для оценки качества работы предложен Tabular Embedding Benchmark (TabBench) — комплексный набор тестов для проверки способности моделей понимать табличные данные. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что TabEmbed значительно превосходит существующие text embedding models и устанавливает новый стандарт для универсального представления табличной информации.

en.title: Unifying Tabular Tasks with TabEmbed
en.desc: TabEmbed is a new embedding model designed to handle both tabular classification and retrieval tasks by using a shared embedding space. It employs large-scale contrastive learning techniques, specifically positive-aware hard negative mining, to improve the model's ability to understand the structure and semantics of tabular data. The introduction of the Tabular Embedding Benchmark (TabBench) allows for a comprehensive evaluation of how well embedding models can interpret tabular information. Experimental results show that TabEmbed outperforms existing text embedding models, setting a new standard for tabular representation learning.

zh.title: TabEmbed：统一表格分类与检索的嵌入模型
zh.desc: 本文介绍了一种新的通用嵌入模型TabEmbed，该模型通过大规模对比学习和正向关注的困难负样本挖掘，将表格分类和检索任务统一在一个共享的嵌入空间中。现有方法在处理表格数据时存在基本局限性，LLM基础的方法缺乏与检索兼容的向量输出，而文本嵌入模型往往无法捕捉表格结构和数值语义。为了解决这个问题，本文首先引入了表格嵌入基准（TabBench），用于评估嵌入模型的表格理解能力。实验结果表明，TabEmbed在TabBench上的表现显著优于现有的文本嵌入模型，为通用表格表示学习建立了新的基准。

papers.title: A^2TGPO: Agentic Turn-Group Policy Optimization with Adaptive Turn-level Clipping
papers.abstract: Abstract Reinforcement learning for agentic LLMs suffers from sparse rewards and challenges in credit assignment, which are addressed through A²TGPO that adapts information gain normalization, accumulation, and clipping for improved policy optimization.  AI-generated summary Reinforcement learning for agentic large language models (LLMs) typically relies on a sparse, trajectory-level outcome reward, making it difficult to evaluate the contribution of individual tool-calls within multi-turn interactions. Existing approaches to such process credit assignment either depend on separate external process reward models that introduce additional consumption, or tree-based structural rollout that merely redistributes the outcome signal while constraining trajectory diversity. A promising alternative leverages the per-turn change in the policy's predicted probability of the ground-truth, termed Information Gain (IG), as an intrinsic process signal without an external evaluator. However, prior work on leveraging IG signals within the RL training loop faces three systematic challenges: normalizing across turns that face heterogeneous positional contexts can distort the relative standing of individual turns, accumulating a variable number of terms causes advantage magnitudes to drift with trajectory depth, and a fixed clipping range governs policy updates identically for turns with vastly different IG signals. In this paper, we propose A^2TGPO (Agentic Turn-Group Policy Optimization with Adaptive Turn-level Clipping), which retains IG as the intrinsic signal but re-designs how it is normalized, accumulated, and consumed: (i) turn-group normalization: normalizes IG within each (prompt, turn-index) group so that each turn is compared only against peers at the same interaction depth; (ii) variance-rescaled discounted accumulation: divides cumulative normalized IG by square root of accumulated terms to keep advantage magnitudes comparable across turn positions; and (iii) adaptive turn-level clipping: modulates each turn's clipping range based on its normalized IG, widening the update region for informative turns and narrowing it for uninformative ones.
affiliations: The Chinese University of Hong Kong Shenzhen

ru.title: Адаптивная оптимизация политики через информационный прирост в агентических LLM
ru.desc: В статье предложен метод A²TGPO для обучения с подкреплением агентических LLM, который решает проблему разреженных наград и приписания кредита. Вместо использования внешних моделей вознаграждения авторы применяют информационный прирост (Information Gain) как внутренний сигнал процесса для оценки вклада каждого шага взаимодействия. Метод включает три ключевых компонента: нормализацию информационного прироста внутри групп поворотов с одинаковой глубиной взаимодействия, масштабирование дисперсией при накоплении для стабилизации величин преимуществ и адаптивное отсечение диапазонов обновлений в зависимости от информативности шага. Это позволяет более эффективно оптимизировать политику в многошаговых диалоговых взаимодействиях.

en.title: Enhancing Learning in LLMs with Adaptive Policy Optimization
en.desc: This paper addresses the challenges of reinforcement learning in agentic large language models (LLMs), particularly the issues of sparse rewards and credit assignment. It introduces A²TGPO, a novel approach that enhances policy optimization by adapting how information gain is normalized, accumulated, and clipped. The method focuses on using intrinsic signals from the model's predictions to improve learning without relying on external evaluators. By implementing turn-group normalization, variance-rescaled accumulation, and adaptive clipping, A²TGPO effectively improves the evaluation of individual contributions in multi-turn interactions.

zh.title: 自适应信息增益优化大语言模型策略
zh.desc: 本论文提出了一种新的强化学习方法A²TGPO，旨在解决大语言模型（LLMs）在稀疏奖励和信用分配方面的挑战。该方法通过自适应的回合级别剪切、信息增益归一化和累积来优化策略。A²TGPO的关键在于将信息增益作为内在信号，避免了依赖外部评估者的问题。通过对每个回合的归一化和调整，A²TGPO能够更有效地评估和优化多轮交互中的策略表现。

papers.title: ReflectDrive-2: Reinforcement-Learning-Aligned Self-Editing for Discrete Diffusion Driving
papers.abstract: Abstract ReflectDrive-2 employs a masked discrete diffusion planner with parallel decoding for autonomous driving, enabling in-place trajectory revision through token rewriting and achieving high performance with efficient reflective decoding.  AI-generated summary We introduce ReflectDrive-2, a masked discrete diffusion planner with separate action expert for autonomous driving that represents plans as discrete trajectory tokens and generates them through parallel masked decoding. This discrete token space enables in-place trajectory revision: AutoEdit rewrites selected tokens using the same model, without requiring an auxiliary refinement network. To train this capability, we use a two-stage procedure. First, we construct structure-aware perturbations of expert trajectories along longitudinal progress and lateral heading directions and supervise the model to recover the original expert trajectory. We then fine-tune the full decision--draft--reflect rollout with reinforcement learning (RL), assigning terminal driving reward to the final post-edit trajectory and propagating policy-gradient credit through full-rollout transitions. Full-rollout RL proves crucial for coupling drafting and editing: under supervised training alone, inference-time AutoEdit improves PDMS by at most 0.3, whereas RL increases its gain to 1.9. We also co-design an efficient reflective decoding stack for the decision--draft--reflect pipeline, combining shared-prefix KV reuse, Alternating Step Decode, and fused on-device unmasking. On NAVSIM, ReflectDrive-2 achieves 91.0 PDMS with camera-only input and 94.8 PDMS in a best-of-6 oracle setting, while running at 31.8 ms average latency on NVIDIA Thor.

ru.title: Диффузионное планирование с редактируемыми траекториями для автономного вождения
ru.desc: ReflectDrive-2 представляет собой планировщик на основе дискретной диффузионной модели для автономного вождения, который кодирует траектории в виде дискретных токенов и генерирует их параллельным декодированием с маской. Модель поддерживает редактирование траекторий на месте через механизм AutoEdit, переписывая отдельные токены без использования дополнительной сети уточнения. Для обучения способности редактирования применяется двухэтапная процедура: сначала модель обучается восстанавливать исходные экспертные траектории из структурированных возмущений, затем проводится обучение с подкреплением на полных развертываниях для связи этапов черновика и редактирования. Эффективный стек декодирования с переиспользованием KV-кэша и слитыми операциями обеспечивает низкую задержку при высокой точности планирования.

en.title: Revolutionizing Autonomous Driving with ReflectDrive-2
en.desc: ReflectDrive-2 is a novel approach for autonomous driving that utilizes a masked discrete diffusion planner to enhance trajectory planning. It represents driving plans as discrete tokens, allowing for efficient in-place trajectory revisions through a process called AutoEdit, which rewrites selected tokens without needing additional networks. The model is trained using a two-stage method that combines supervised learning with reinforcement learning (RL) to improve the quality of the final driving trajectory. This approach not only boosts performance metrics significantly but also ensures efficient decoding during the planning process, achieving high accuracy with low latency.

zh.title: 高效的自动驾驶轨迹规划与修订
zh.desc: ReflectDrive-2 是一种用于自动驾驶的掩码离散扩散规划器，采用并行解码技术。它通过将计划表示为离散轨迹标记，实现了在轨迹修订时的就地修改。该模型使用两阶段训练程序，首先构建专家轨迹的结构感知扰动，然后通过强化学习微调决策-草拟-反思的流程。在NAVSIM上，ReflectDrive-2 在仅使用摄像头输入时达到了91.0的PDMS，展现了其高效的性能。

papers.title: UniPool: A Globally Shared Expert Pool for Mixture-of-Experts
papers.abstract: Abstract UniPool introduces a shared expert pool architecture for Mixture-of-Experts models that reduces parameter growth with depth while maintaining or improving performance through balanced training and stable routing mechanisms.  AI-generated summary Modern Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architectures allocate expert capacity through a rigid per-layer rule: each transformer layer owns a separate expert set. This convention couples depth scaling with linear expert-parameter growth and assumes that every layer needs isolated expert capacity. However, recent analyses and our routing probe challenge this allocation rule: replacing a deeper layer's learned top-k router with uniform random routing drops downstream accuracy by only 1.0-1.6 points across multiple production MoE models. Motivated by this redundancy, we propose UniPool, an MoE architecture that treats expert capacity as a global architectural budget by replacing per-layer expert ownership with a single shared pool accessed by independent per-layer routers. To enable stable and balanced training under sharing, we introduce a pool-level auxiliary loss that balances expert utilization across the entire pool, and adopt NormRouter to provide sparse and scale-stable routing into the shared expert pool. Across five LLaMA-architecture model scales (182M, 469M, 650M, 830M, and 978M parameters) trained on 30B tokens from the Pile, UniPool consistently improves validation loss and perplexity over the matched vanilla MoE baselines. Across these scales, UniPool reduces validation loss by up to 0.0386 relative to vanilla MoE. Beyond raw loss improvement, our results identify pool size as an explicit depth-scaling hyperparameter: reduced-pool UniPool variants using only 41.6%-66.7% of the vanilla expert-parameter budget match or outperform layer-wise MoE at the tested scales. This shows that, under a shared-pool design, expert parameters need not grow linearly with depth; they can grow sublinearly while remaining more efficient and effective than vanilla MoE. Further analysis shows that UniPool's benefits compose with finer-grained expert decomposition.
affiliations: The Chinese University of Hong Kong

ru.title: Один пул экспертов вместо слоя за слоем
ru.desc: В статье предлагается UniPool — архитектура Mixture-of-Experts (MoE), которая использует единый общий пул экспертов вместо отдельного набора экспертов на каждом слое трансформера. Это позволяет снизить рост параметров при увеличении глубины модели, так как авторы показали, что не все слои нуждаются в изолированной экспертной ёмкости. Для стабильного обучения при совместном использовании пула они вводят вспомогательную функцию потерь на уровне пула и применяют NormRouter для сбалансированной маршрутизации. Результаты демонстрируют улучшение качества при одновременном снижении требуемого количества параметров экспертов на 33-58% по сравнению со стандартной архитектурой MoE.

en.title: UniPool: Efficient Expert Sharing for Enhanced MoE Performance
en.desc: UniPool presents a new architecture for Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models that utilizes a shared pool of experts instead of assigning separate experts to each layer. This approach reduces the number of parameters needed as the model gets deeper, while still improving performance through better training and routing strategies. By implementing a pool-level auxiliary loss, UniPool ensures that expert usage is balanced across the entire pool, leading to more efficient training. The results demonstrate that UniPool can achieve lower validation loss and perplexity compared to traditional MoE models, showing that expert parameters can grow at a slower rate without sacrificing effectiveness.

zh.title: 共享专家池，提升混合专家模型的效率
zh.desc: UniPool提出了一种共享专家池架构，用于混合专家模型，能够在深度增加时减少参数增长，同时通过平衡训练和稳定路由机制来保持或提高性能。传统的混合专家架构为每一层分配独立的专家集，这导致了参数的线性增长。UniPool通过引入一个共享的专家池，允许各层独立的路由器访问同一池中的专家，从而优化了专家的利用率。实验结果表明，UniPool在多个模型规模上均能显著降低验证损失，并且在参数预算上实现了更高的效率。

papers.title: RaguTeam at SemEval-2026 Task 8: Meno and Friends in a Judge-Orchestrated LLM Ensemble for Faithful Multi-Turn Response Generation
papers.abstract: Abstract A heterogeneous ensemble of seven large language models with dual prompting strategies achieved top performance in the SemEval-2026 MTRAGEval task through judge selection and demonstrated the importance of model diversity.  AI-generated summary We present our winning system for Task~B (generation with reference passages) in SemEval-2026 Task~8: MTRAGEval. Our method is a heterogeneous ensemble of seven LLMs with two prompting variants, where a GPT-4o-mini judge selects the best candidate per instance. We ranked 1st out of 26 teams, achieving a conditioned harmonic mean of 0.7827 and outperforming the strongest baseline (gpt-oss-120b, 0.6390). Ablations show that diversity in model families, scales, and prompting strategies is essential, with the ensemble consistently beating any single model. We also introduce Meno-Lite-0.1, a 7B domain-adapted model with a strong cost--performance trade-off, and analyse MTRAGEval, highlighting annotation limitations and directions for improvement. Our code is publicly available: https://github.com/RaguTeam/ragu_mtrag_semeval

ru.title: Сила разнообразия: ансамбль LLM побеждает через стратегическое комбинирование моделей
ru.desc: Авторы представляют систему на основе гетерогенного ансамбля из семи больших языковых моделей с двумя вариантами промптинга для задачи генерации текста с использованием справочных документов в соревновании SemEval-2026. Ключевая инновация заключается в применении механизма выбора судьи на основе GPT-4o-mini, который для каждого примера выбирает лучший вариант ответа из сгенерированных кандидатов. Исследование демонстрирует критическую важность разнообразия в составе моделей, их размерах и стратегиях промптинга, показывая, что ансамбль систематически превосходит любую отдельную модель. Авторы также представляют адаптированную для конкретной области модель Meno-Lite-0.1 размером 7 млрд параметров с оптимальным соотношением качества и вычислительной стоимости.

en.title: Harnessing Diversity: Winning with Ensemble Models in MTRAGEval
en.desc: This paper presents a successful approach to the SemEval-2026 MTRAGEval task using a heterogeneous ensemble of seven large language models (LLMs). The ensemble employs dual prompting strategies and utilizes a GPT-4o-mini judge to select the best output for each instance, demonstrating the effectiveness of model diversity. The team achieved the highest score among 26 competitors, significantly surpassing the strongest baseline model. Additionally, the paper introduces Meno-Lite-0.1, a cost-effective domain-adapted model, and discusses the limitations of the MTRAGEval annotations, suggesting areas for future improvement.

zh.title: 模型多样性助力最佳表现
zh.desc: 本论文介绍了一种异构集成方法，结合了七个大型语言模型和双重提示策略，在SemEval-2026 MTRAGEval任务中取得了最佳表现。我们的方法通过使用GPT-4o-mini评判者选择每个实例的最佳候选，展示了模型多样性的重要性。实验结果表明，模型家族、规模和提示策略的多样性是成功的关键，集成模型的表现始终优于任何单一模型。我们还提出了Meno-Lite-0.1，这是一个具有良好性价比的7B领域适应模型，并分析了MTRAGEval的注释局限性及改进方向。

papers.title: SwiftI2V: Efficient High-Resolution Image-to-Video Generation via Conditional Segment-wise Generation
papers.abstract: Abstract SwiftI2V is an efficient high-resolution image-to-video generation framework that uses conditional segment-wise generation and bidirectional contextual interaction to achieve scalable, input-faithful video synthesis with reduced computational requirements.  AI-generated summary High-resolution image-to-video (I2V) generation aims to synthesize realistic temporal dynamics while preserving fine-grained appearance details of the input image. At 2K resolution, it becomes extremely challenging, and existing solutions suffer from various weaknesses: 1) end-to-end models are often prohibitively expensive in memory and latency; 2) cascading low-resolution generation with a generic video super-resolution tends to hallucinate details and drift from input-specific local structures, since the super-resolution stage is not explicitly conditioned on the input image. To this end, we propose SwiftI2V, an efficient framework tailored for high-resolution I2V. Following the widely used two-stage design, it addresses the efficiency--fidelity dilemma by first generating a low-resolution motion reference to reduce token costs and ease the modeling burden, then performing a strongly image-conditioned 2K synthesis guided by the motion to recover input-faithful details with controlled overhead. Specifically, to make generation more scalable, SwiftI2V introduces Conditional Segment-wise Generation (CSG) to synthesize videos segment-by-segment with a bounded per-step token budget, and adopts bidirectional contextual interaction within each segment to improve cross-segment coherence and input fidelity. On VBench-I2V at 2K resolution, SwiftI2V achieves performance comparable to end-to-end baselines while reducing total GPU-time by 202x. Particularly, it enables practical 2K I2V generation on a single datacenter GPU (e.g., H800) or consumer GPU (e.g., RTX 4090).

ru.title: Эффективная трансформация изображения в видео высокого разрешения через поегментную генерацию
ru.desc: SwiftI2V — это эффективная система для генерации видео из изображения с разрешением 2K, которая использует двухэтапный подход: сначала генерируется низкоразрешённый видеоряд с движением, затем выполняется синтез высокого разрешения с сильной обусловленностью на исходное изображение. Для масштабируемости метод применяет условную поегментную генерацию (CSG), разбивая видео на сегменты с ограниченным бюджетом токенов на каждом шаге. Двусторонний контекстный обмен внутри каждого сегмента улучшает согласованность между сегментами и верность исходному изображению. Система достигает производительности, сравнимой с end-to-end моделями, при этом сокращая вычислительные затраты в 202 раза, что делает практичной генерацию видео на одном GPU.

en.title: Efficient High-Resolution Image-to-Video Generation with SwiftI2V
en.desc: SwiftI2V is a novel framework designed for generating high-resolution videos from images efficiently. It utilizes a two-stage approach, first creating a low-resolution motion reference to minimize computational costs, followed by a high-fidelity synthesis that maintains the details of the original image. The framework introduces Conditional Segment-wise Generation (CSG) to produce videos in segments, ensuring that each segment adheres to a limited token budget while enhancing coherence across segments. By implementing these strategies, SwiftI2V significantly reduces GPU time while achieving performance on par with traditional end-to-end models, making high-resolution image-to-video generation more accessible.

zh.title: 高效的图像到视频生成框架
zh.desc: SwiftI2V 是一个高效的高分辨率图像到视频生成框架，采用条件分段生成和双向上下文交互来实现可扩展的、忠实于输入的视频合成，同时减少计算需求。该框架通过先生成低分辨率的运动参考来降低计算成本，然后在此基础上进行强图像条件的 2K 合成，以恢复输入图像的细节。SwiftI2V 引入了条件分段生成（CSG），逐段合成视频，并在每个段内采用双向上下文交互，以提高跨段一致性和输入保真度。在 VBench-I2V 上，SwiftI2V 在 2K 分辨率下的性能与端到端基线相当，同时将总 GPU 时间减少了 202 倍。

papers.title: RemoteZero: Geospatial Reasoning with Zero Human Annotations
papers.abstract: Abstract RemoteZero enables geospatial reasoning without box supervision by leveraging semantic verification capabilities of MLLMs for self-evolving localization from unlabeled remote sensing data.  AI-generated summary Geospatial reasoning requires models to resolve complex spatial semantics and user intent into precise target locations for Earth observation. Recent progress has liberated the reasoning path from manual curation, allowing models to generate their own inference chains. Yet a final dependency remains: they are still supervised by human-annotated ground-truth coordinates. This leaves the reasoning process autonomous, but not its spatial endpoint, and prevents true self-evolution on abundant unlabeled remote sensing data. To break this bottleneck, we introduce RemoteZero, a box-supervision-free framework for geospatial reasoning. RemoteZero is motivated by a simple asymmetry: an MLLM is typically better at verifying whether a region satisfies a query than at directly generating precise coordinates. Leveraging this stronger discriminative ability, RemoteZero replaces geometric supervision with intrinsic semantic verification and enables GRPO training without box annotations. The resulting framework further supports iterative self-evolution, allowing the model to improve from unlabeled remote sensing imagery through its own verification signal. Experiments show that RemoteZero achieves competitive performance against strong supervised methods, demonstrating the potential of self-verifying training for geospatial reasoning localization.

ru.title: Самоэволюция геолокализации через семантическую верификацию без разметки
ru.desc: RemoteZero — это фреймворк для геопространственного рассуждения, который избегает необходимости разметки координат благодаря использованию многомодальных языковых моделей. Вместо традиционного подхода с человеческой разметкой целевых координат, метод применяет семантическую верификацию — проверку соответствия региона запросу. Фреймворк использует метод GRPO (Group Relative Policy Optimization) для обучения моделей на неразмеченных спутниковых снимках, позволяя моделям эволюционировать самостоятельно через собственные сигналы верификации. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что RemoteZero достигает конкурентной производительности с полностью супервизированными методами.

en.title: Empowering Geospatial Reasoning Without Box Supervision
en.desc: RemoteZero is a novel framework that enhances geospatial reasoning by eliminating the need for box supervision in localization tasks. It utilizes the semantic verification capabilities of Multi-Layered Language Models (MLLMs) to autonomously refine its understanding of spatial semantics from unlabeled remote sensing data. By focusing on verifying whether a region meets a specific query instead of generating exact coordinates, RemoteZero allows for self-evolving training processes. This approach not only improves the model's performance but also demonstrates the effectiveness of self-verifying training in achieving competitive results against traditional supervised methods.

zh.title: RemoteZero：无框监督的地理空间推理新方法
zh.desc: RemoteZero 是一个无需框架监督的地理空间推理框架，利用 MLLM 的语义验证能力，从未标记的遥感数据中实现自我演化的定位。该方法解决了传统地理空间推理中对人工标注坐标的依赖，使得模型能够自主生成推理链。通过替代几何监督，RemoteZero 采用内在的语义验证，支持无框注释的 GRPO 训练。实验结果表明，RemoteZero 在性能上与强监督方法相当，展示了自我验证训练在地理空间推理定位中的潜力。

papers.title: SkillOS: Learning Skill Curation for Self-Evolving Agents
papers.abstract: Abstract SkillOS enables self-evolving LLM agents to learn complex long-term skill curation policies through reinforcement learning, improving performance across diverse tasks while generalizing across different executor architectures.  AI-generated summary LLM-based agents are increasingly deployed to handle streaming tasks, yet they often remain one-off problem solvers that fail to learn from past interactions. Reusable skills distilled from experience provide a natural substrate for self-evolution, where high-quality skill curation serves as the key bottleneck. Existing approaches either rely on manual skill curation, prescribe heuristic skill operations, or train for short-horizon skill operations. However, they still struggle to learn complex long-term curation policies from indirect and delayed feedback. To tackle this challenge, we propose SkillOS, an experience-driven RL training recipe for learning skill curation in self-evolving agents. SkillOS pairs a frozen agent executor that retrieves and applies skills with a trainable skill curator that updates an external SkillRepo from accumulated experience. To provide learning signals for curation, we design composite rewards and train on grouped task streams based on skill-relevant task dependencies, where earlier trajectories update the SkillRepo, and later related tasks evaluate these updates. Across multi-turn agentic tasks and single-turn reasoning tasks, SkillOS consistently outperforms memory-free and strong memory-based baselines in both effectiveness and efficiency, with the learned skill curator generalizing across different executor backbones and task domains. Further analyses show that the learned curator produces more targeted skill use, while the skills in SkillRepo evolve into more richly structured Markdown files that encode higher-level meta-skills over time.

ru.title: Самоэволюция навыков: обучение с подкреплением для интеллектуальных агентов
ru.desc: SkillOS — это система для самоэволюционирующих LLM-агентов, которая использует обучение с подкреплением для изучения политик курирования сложных долгосрочных навыков. Система состоит из замороженного исполнителя, который применяет навыки, и обучаемого куратора навыков, который обновляет репозиторий на основе накопленного опыта. Для обучения используются составные награды и группировка потоков задач по зависимостям, связанным с навыками, что позволяет модели учиться на основе косвенной и отложенной обратной связи. Экспериментально доказано, что SkillOS превосходит базовые методы по эффективности и обобщаемости на разные архитектуры исполнителей и домены задач.

en.title: Empowering LLM Agents with Self-Evolving Skill Curation
en.desc: SkillOS is a framework that allows large language model (LLM) agents to continuously improve their ability to curate skills through reinforcement learning. It addresses the limitation of traditional agents that only solve problems without learning from past experiences. By using a frozen executor to apply skills and a trainable curator to update a SkillRepo, SkillOS enables agents to learn from indirect feedback and adapt their skill usage over time. This approach leads to better performance in various tasks and allows the skill curator to generalize across different architectures and domains.

zh.title: SkillOS：自我进化的技能管理新方法
zh.desc: SkillOS 是一种自我进化的 LLM 代理，通过强化学习学习复杂的长期技能管理策略，从而在多样化任务中提高性能并在不同执行架构中实现泛化。现有的 LLM 代理通常只能解决一次性问题，无法从过去的交互中学习。SkillOS 通过经验驱动的强化学习训练方法，结合固定的代理执行器和可训练的技能管理器，来更新外部技能库，从而解决这一挑战。实验结果表明，SkillOS 在多轮和单轮任务中均优于传统的无记忆和强记忆基线，且学习到的技能管理器能够在不同的执行框架和任务领域中泛化。

papers.title: Can RL Teach Long-Horizon Reasoning to LLMs? Expressiveness Is Key
papers.abstract: Abstract ScaleLogic demonstrates that reinforcement learning training compute scales as a power law with reasoning depth, with scaling exponents increasing monotonically with logical expressiveness across multiple reasoning tasks.  AI-generated summary Reinforcement learning (RL) has been applied to improve large language model (LLM) reasoning, yet the systematic study of how training scales with task difficulty has been hampered by the lack of controlled, scalable environments. We introduce ScaleLogic, a synthetic logical reasoning framework that offers independent control over two axes of difficulty: the depth of the required proof planning (i.e., the horizon) and the expressiveness of the underlying logic. Our proposed framework supports a wide range of logics: from simple implication-only logic ("if-then") towards more expressive first-order reasoning with conjunction ("and"), disjunction ("or"), negation ("not"), and universal quantification ("for all"). Using this framework, we show that the RL training compute T follows a power law with respect to reasoning depth D (T propto D^γ, R^{2} > 0.99), and that the scaling exponent γ increases monotonically with logical expressiveness, from 1.04 to 2.60. On downstream mathematics and general reasoning benchmarks, more expressive training settings yield both larger performance gains (up to +10.66 points) and more compute-efficient transfer compared to less expressive settings, demonstrating that what a model is trained on, not just how much it is trained, shapes downstream transfer. We further show that the power-law relationship holds across multiple RL methods, and curriculum-based training substantially improves scaling efficiency.

ru.title: Степенной закон масштабирования обучения с подкреплением в зависимости от логической сложности
ru.desc: В статье представлена ScaleLogic — синтетическая среда для изучения логических рассуждений, которая позволяет независимо контролировать глубину доказательства и выразительность логики. Авторы показывают, что объём вычислительных ресурсов для обучения с подкреплением масштабируется как степенной закон в зависимости от глубины рассуждений, причём показатель степени растёт с увеличением логической выразительности задач. Исследование демонстрирует, что более выразительные логические системы обеспечивают лучший перенос знаний на задачи математического и общего рассуждения, и что учебный план обучения существенно улучшает эффективность масштабирования. Результаты подтверждают, что не только объём обучающих данных, но и их качество и сложность определяют способность модели к обобщению.

en.title: Scaling Reinforcement Learning with Logical Reasoning Complexity
en.desc: The paper introduces ScaleLogic, a framework for studying how reinforcement learning (RL) training scales with the complexity of logical reasoning tasks. It shows that the amount of compute needed for training follows a power law based on the depth of reasoning required, meaning that as tasks become more complex, the compute required increases significantly. The research highlights that more expressive logical frameworks lead to better performance in downstream tasks, indicating that the type of training is crucial for effective learning. Additionally, the findings suggest that using a structured approach to training can enhance efficiency in scaling RL models.

zh.title: 强化学习训练与推理深度的幂律关系
zh.desc: ScaleLogic展示了强化学习训练的计算需求与推理深度之间遵循幂律关系，并且随着逻辑表达能力的增强，幂律指数单调增加。我们提出的ScaleLogic框架允许独立控制推理任务的两个难度轴：所需证明规划的深度和基础逻辑的表达能力。通过这个框架，我们发现训练计算需求与推理深度之间的关系可以用幂律公式表示，并且更具表达能力的训练设置在下游任务中表现出更大的性能提升和更高的计算效率。我们的研究表明，模型的训练内容而不仅仅是训练量，决定了其在下游任务中的迁移能力。

papers.title: The Granularity Axis: A Micro-to-Macro Latent Direction for Social Roles in Language Models
papers.abstract: Abstract Large language models encode social role granularity as a structured latent dimension that can be manipulated through activation steering, demonstrating consistent patterns across different model architectures and prompting conditions.  AI-generated summary Large language models (LLMs) are routinely prompted to take on social roles ranging from individuals to institutions, yet it remains unclear whether their internal representations encode the granularity of such roles, from micro-level individual experience to macro-level organizational, institutional, or national reasoning. We show that they do. We define a contrast-based Granularity Axis as the difference between mean macro- and micro-role hidden states. In Qwen3-8B, this axis aligns with the principal axis (PC1) of the role representation space at cosine 0.972 and accounts for 52.6% of its variance, indicating that granularity is the dominant geometric axis organizing prompted social roles. We construct 75 social roles across five granularity levels and collect 91,200 role-conditioned responses over shared questions and prompt variants, then extract role-level hidden states and project them onto the axis. Role projections increase monotonically across all five levels, remain stable across layers, prompt variants, endpoint definitions, held-out splits, and score-filtered subsets, and transfer to Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct. The axis is also causally relevant: activation steering along it shifts response granularity in the predicted direction, with Llama moving from 2.00 to 3.17 on a five-point macro scale under positive steering on prompts that admit local responses. The two models differ in controllability, suggesting that steering depends on each model's default operating regime. Overall, our findings suggest that social role granularity is not merely a stylistic surface feature, but a structured, ordered, and causally manipulable latent direction in role-conditioned language model behavior.

ru.title: Социальная роль как управляемое скрытое измерение в языковых моделях
ru.desc: В этой работе авторы показали, что большие языковые модели кодируют уровень детализации социальных ролей как структурированное скрытое измерение в своих внутренних представлениях. Исследователи определили ось гранулярности как разницу между средними скрытыми состояниями макро- и микро-ролей и обнаружили, что она является доминирующей геометрической осью в пространстве представлений ролей. Они протестировали это на 75 социальных ролях, разделённых на пять уровней детализации, и показали, что проекции ролей монотонно возрастают вдоль оси, оставаясь стабильными через слои модели и переносясь между разными архитектурами. Кроме того, авторы продемонстрировали каузальную релевантность оси: управление активациями вдоль неё успешно смещает ответы модели от микро-уровня к макро-уровню и обратно.

en.title: Unlocking Social Role Granularity in Language Models
en.desc: This paper explores how large language models (LLMs) represent social roles with varying levels of detail, from individual experiences to broader institutional perspectives. It introduces a concept called the Granularity Axis, which quantifies the difference between micro-level and macro-level role representations. The study demonstrates that this axis is a significant factor in organizing social roles within the model, accounting for a large portion of variance in role representation. Additionally, the research shows that manipulating this axis through activation steering can effectively change the model's responses, indicating that social role granularity is a structured and controllable aspect of LLM behavior.

zh.title: 社会角色细粒度的潜在操控
zh.desc: 这篇论文探讨了大型语言模型如何编码社会角色的细粒度，并通过激活引导进行操控。研究表明，这些模型的内部表示能够反映从个体经验到组织、机构或国家推理的不同层次。作者定义了一个对比基础的细粒度轴，显示出在不同模型架构和提示条件下，社会角色的表现具有一致的模式。结果表明，社会角色的细粒度不仅是表面特征，而是可以结构化、排序和因果操控的潜在方向。

papers.title: Are We Making Progress in Multimodal Domain Generalization? A Comprehensive Benchmark Study
papers.abstract: Abstract MMDG-Bench presents a unified benchmark for multimodal domain generalization that standardizes evaluation across diverse tasks and modalities while revealing limited performance gains and significant robustness challenges.  AI-generated summary Despite the growing popularity of Multimodal Domain Generalization (MMDG) for enhancing model robustness, it remains unclear whether reported performance gains reflect genuine algorithmic progress or are artifacts of inconsistent evaluation protocols. Current research is fragmented, with studies varying significantly across datasets, modality configurations, and experimental settings. Furthermore, existing benchmarks focus predominantly on action recognition, often neglecting critical real-world challenges such as input corruptions, missing modalities, and model trustworthiness. This lack of standardization obscures a reliable assessment of the field's advancement. To address this issue, we introduce MMDG-Bench, the first unified and comprehensive benchmark for MMDG, which standardizes evaluation across six datasets spanning three diverse tasks: action recognition, mechanical fault diagnosis, and sentiment analysis. MMDG-Bench encompasses six modality combinations, nine representative methods, and multiple evaluation settings. Beyond standard accuracy, it systematically assesses corruption robustness, missing-modality generalization, misclassification detection, and out-of-distribution detection. With 7, 402 neural networks trained in total across 95 unique cross-domain tasks, MMDG-Bench yields five key findings: (1) under fair comparisons, recent specialized MMDG methods offer only marginal improvements over ERM baseline; (2) no single method consistently outperforms others across datasets or modality combinations; (3) a substantial gap to upper-bound performance persists, indicating that MMDG remains far from solved; (4) trimodal fusion does not consistently outperform the strongest bimodal configurations; and (5) all evaluated methods exhibit significant degradation under corruption and missing-modality scenarios, with some methods further compromising model trustworthiness.

ru.title: Когда добавление модальностей не гарантирует успех
ru.desc: В статье представлен MMDG-Bench — первый унифицированный бенчмарк для мультимодальной обобщаемости моделей на новых доменах, который стандартизирует оценку алгоритмов по шести датасетам и трём типам задач. Исследование показывает, что специализированные методы дают лишь незначительные улучшения по сравнению с базовым методом ERM, и ни один алгоритм не доминирует последовательно на всех конфигурациях модальностей. Авторы выявили серьёзные проблемы с робастностью: все протестированные методы значительно деградируют при повреждениях входных данных и отсутствии модальностей, некоторые снижают надёжность модели. Результаты свидетельствуют о том, что мультимодальная обобщаемость остаётся далеко не решённой проблемой и требует более глубокого исследования.

en.title: Standardizing Multimodal Domain Generalization Evaluation
en.desc: The paper introduces MMDG-Bench, a standardized benchmark for evaluating Multimodal Domain Generalization (MMDG) across various tasks and modalities. It highlights the challenges in assessing model performance due to inconsistent evaluation protocols and fragmented research. The benchmark includes six datasets and evaluates multiple aspects such as robustness to input corruption and missing modalities. Key findings reveal that recent MMDG methods show only slight improvements over traditional methods, indicating that the field still has significant challenges to address.

zh.title: 统一评估，多模态领域泛化的新标准
zh.desc: MMDG-Bench是一个统一的基准测试，旨在评估多模态领域泛化的性能。它标准化了在六个数据集上的评估，涵盖了动作识别、机械故障诊断和情感分析等三种任务。研究发现，尽管一些专门的多模态方法在准确性上有所提升，但与传统的ERM基线相比，进步非常有限。此外，所有评估的方法在输入损坏和缺失模态的情况下表现不佳，显示出模型的鲁棒性和可信度仍然是一个重要挑战。

papers.title: Skill1: Unified Evolution of Skill-Augmented Agents via Reinforcement Learning
papers.abstract: Abstract Skill1 is a unified framework that trains a single policy to simultaneously evolve skill selection, utilization, and distillation capabilities using a shared task-outcome objective, demonstrating superior performance over existing baselines in complex task environments.  AI-generated summary A persistent skill library allows language model agents to reuse successful strategies across tasks. Maintaining such a library requires three coupled capabilities. The agent selects a relevant skill, utilizes it during execution, and distills new skills from experience. Existing methods optimize these capabilities in isolation or with separate reward sources, resulting in partial and conflicting evolution. We propose Skill1, a framework that trains a single policy to co-evolve skill selection, utilization, and distillation toward a shared task-outcome objective. The policy generates a query to search the skill library, re-ranks candidates to select one, solves the task conditioned on it, and distills a new skill from the trajectory. All learning derives from a single task-outcome signal. Its low-frequency trend credits selection and its high-frequency variation credits distillation. Experiments on ALFWorld and WebShop show that Skill1 outperforms prior skill-based and reinforcement learning baselines. Training dynamics confirm the co-evolution of the three capabilities, and ablations show that removing any credit signal degrades the evolution.
affiliations: University of Science and Technology of China

ru.title: Единая политика для совместного развития отбора, применения и дистилляции навыков
ru.desc: Skill1 — это единая система, которая обучает одну политику одновременно развивать способности отбора навыков, их применения и создания новых навыков из опыта, используя общую целевую функцию на основе результатов задач. Система хранит библиотеку переиспользуемых стратегий, которые агент может применять к различным задачам: он ищет подходящий навык, выбирает лучший кандидат, выполняет задачу с его помощью и генерирует новые навыки из полученной траектории. Вместо отдельной оптимизации каждой способности, все обучение происходит от единого сигнала обратной связи, где низкочастотные тренды направляют отбор навыков, а высокочастотные вариации — их совершенствование. Эксперименты показывают превосходство Skill1 над существующими методами на сложных задачах в средах ALFWorld и WebShop.

en.title: Unified Skill Evolution for Enhanced AI Performance
en.desc: Skill1 is a novel framework designed for training AI agents to effectively manage skills in complex tasks. It integrates three key capabilities: skill selection, skill utilization, and skill distillation, all driven by a unified task-outcome objective. Unlike previous methods that treat these capabilities separately, Skill1 allows them to evolve together, enhancing overall performance. Experiments demonstrate that this approach leads to superior results compared to existing skill-based and reinforcement learning techniques.

zh.title: Skill1：统一技能进化框架
zh.desc: Skill1是一个统一的框架，旨在通过共享的任务结果目标同时训练技能选择、利用和提炼能力。该框架允许语言模型代理在复杂任务环境中重用成功的策略。Skill1通过单一策略共同进化这三种能力，避免了现有方法中孤立优化导致的部分和冲突的演变。实验结果表明，Skill1在ALFWorld和WebShop上优于以往的技能基础和强化学习基线。

papers.title: AI Co-Mathematician: Accelerating Mathematicians with Agentic AI
papers.abstract: Abstract  We introduce the AI co-mathematician, a workbench for mathematicians to interactively leverage AI agents to pursue open-ended research. The AI co-mathematician is optimized to provide holistic support for the exploratory and iterative reality of mathematical workflows, including ideation, literature search, computational exploration, theorem proving and theory building. By providing an asynchronous, stateful workspace that manages uncertainty, refines user intent, tracks failed hypotheses, and outputs native mathematical artifacts, the system mirrors human collaborative workflows. In early tests, the AI co-mathematician helped researchers solve open problems, identify new research directions, and uncover overlooked literature references. Besides demonstrating a highly interactive paradigm for AI-assisted mathematical discovery, the AI co-mathematician also achieves state of the art results on hard problem-solving benchmarks, including scoring 48% on FrontierMath Tier 4, a new high score among all AI systems evaluated.

ru.title: Интерактивный математический помощник на базе искусственного интеллекта
ru.desc: Представлена система AI co-mathematician, которая помогает математикам в проведении исследований благодаря интеграции агентов на основе больших языковых моделей. Система поддерживает все этапы математического творчества: от генерации идей и поиска литературы до вычислительных экспериментов и доказательства теорем. Платформа использует асинхронный рабочий процесс, управляет неопределённостью, отслеживает неудачные гипотезы и генерирует математические объекты в нативных форматах. При тестировании система помогла исследователям решить открытые проблемы и достигла рекордных результатов на сложных бенчмарках задач.

en.title: Empowering Mathematicians with AI Collaboration
en.desc: The AI co-mathematician is a tool designed to assist mathematicians in their research by using AI agents. It supports various stages of mathematical work, such as brainstorming ideas, searching for relevant literature, exploring computations, proving theorems, and building theories. This system creates a collaborative environment that adapts to user needs, manages uncertainties, and tracks previous attempts, making it similar to how humans work together. In initial tests, it has successfully helped researchers tackle complex problems and discover new areas of study, achieving impressive results on challenging benchmarks.

zh.title: AI共数学家：数学研究的新助手
zh.desc: 我们介绍了AI共数学家，这是一个供数学家互动使用的工作平台，旨在利用AI代理进行开放式研究。AI共数学家优化了数学工作流程的探索性和迭代性，支持创意生成、文献搜索、计算探索、定理证明和理论构建。该系统提供一个异步的、有状态的工作空间，管理不确定性、细化用户意图、跟踪失败的假设，并输出原生数学成果，模拟人类的协作工作流程。在早期测试中，AI共数学家帮助研究人员解决开放性问题，识别新的研究方向，并发现被忽视的文献参考。

papers.title: Recovering Hidden Reward in Diffusion-Based Policies
papers.abstract: Abstract EnergyFlow unifies generative action modeling with inverse reinforcement learning by parameterizing an energy function whose gradient serves as a denoising field, enabling reward extraction without adversarial training while improving policy generalization through structural constraints.  AI-generated summary This paper introduces EnergyFlow, a framework that unifies generative action modeling with inverse reinforcement learning by parameterizing a scalar energy function whose gradient is the denoising field. We establish that under maximum-entropy optimality, the score function learned via denoising score matching recovers the gradient of the expert's soft Q-function, enabling reward extraction without adversarial training. Formally, we prove that constraining the learned field to be conservative reduces hypothesis complexity and tightens out-of-distribution generalization bounds. We further characterize the identifiability of recovered rewards and bound how score estimation errors propagate to action preferences. Empirically, EnergyFlow achieves state-of-the-art imitation performance on various manipulation tasks while providing an effective reward signal for downstream reinforcement learning that outperforms both adversarial IRL methods and likelihood-based alternatives. These results show that the structural constraints required for valid reward extraction simultaneously serve as beneficial inductive biases for policy generalization. The code is available at https://github.com/sotaagi/EnergyFlow.

ru.title: Энергетический поток: единство генеративного моделирования и обучения от примеров
ru.desc: EnergyFlow объединяет генеративное моделирование действий с обратным обучением с подкреплением через параметризацию энергетической функции. Градиент этой функции служит полем деноизирования, позволяя извлекать функцию награды без состязательного обучения. Авторы доказывают, что при условии максимальной энтропийности градиент функции оценки совпадает с градиентом мягкой Q-функции эксперта, что улучшает обобщение за пределами обучающего распределения. Метод демонстрирует передовые результаты в задачах имитационного обучения и генерирует эффективные сигналы награды для дальнейшего обучения с подкреплением.

en.title: EnergyFlow: Unifying Generative Action Modeling and Inverse Reinforcement Learning for Better Policy Generalization
en.desc: EnergyFlow is a novel framework that combines generative action modeling with inverse reinforcement learning by using a scalar energy function. This energy function's gradient acts as a denoising field, allowing for effective reward extraction without the need for adversarial training. The paper demonstrates that applying structural constraints to the learned field enhances policy generalization and reduces complexity in hypothesis testing. Empirical results show that EnergyFlow outperforms existing methods in imitation learning tasks and provides a robust reward signal for reinforcement learning applications.

zh.title: EnergyFlow：无对抗训练的奖励提取新方法
zh.desc: EnergyFlow是一个将生成动作建模与逆强化学习相结合的框架。它通过参数化一个标量能量函数，使得其梯度作为去噪场，从而实现无需对抗训练的奖励提取。研究表明，在最大熵最优性下，通过去噪得分匹配学习的得分函数能够恢复专家的软Q函数的梯度。EnergyFlow在多种操作任务中实现了最先进的模仿性能，同时为后续的强化学习提供了有效的奖励信号。

papers.title: EDU-CIRCUIT-HW: Evaluating Multimodal Large Language Models on Real-World University-Level STEM Student Handwritten Solutions
papers.abstract: Abstract EDU-CIRCUIT-HW dataset reveals significant limitations in MLLMs' ability to accurately interpret complex STEM handwritten solutions, prompting a hybrid approach combining automated recognition with minimal human oversight for improved educational grading systems.  AI-generated summary Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) hold significant promise for revolutionizing traditional education and reducing teachers' workload. However, accurately interpreting unconstrained STEM student handwritten solutions with intertwined mathematical formulas, diagrams, and textual reasoning poses a significant challenge due to the lack of authentic and domain-specific benchmarks. Additionally, current evaluation paradigms predominantly rely on the outcomes of downstream tasks (e.g., auto-grading), which often probe only a subset of the recognized content, thereby failing to capture the MLLMs' understanding of complex handwritten logic as a whole. To bridge this gap, we release EDU-CIRCUIT-HW, a dataset consisting of 1,300+ authentic student handwritten solutions from a university-level STEM course. Utilizing the expert-verified verbatim transcriptions and grading reports of student solutions, we simultaneously evaluate various MLLMs' upstream recognition fidelity and downstream auto-grading performance. Our evaluation uncovers an astonishing scale of latent failures within MLLM-recognized student handwritten content, highlighting the models' insufficient reliability for auto-grading and other understanding-oriented applications in high-stakes educational settings. As a potential solution, we present a case study demonstrating that leveraging identified error patterns to preemptively detect and correct recognition errors, while requiring only minimal human intervention (e.g., routing 3.3% of assignments to human graders and the remainder to the GPT-5.1 grader), can effectively enhance the robustness of the deployed AI-enabled grading system. Code and dataset are available in this GitHub repo: https://gt-learning-innovation.github.io/CIRCUIT_EDU_HW_ACL.

ru.title: Гибридная система оценивания STEM-решений с проверкой человеком для надёжного образовательного AI
ru.desc: В работе представлен датасет EDU-CIRCUIT-HW с более чем 1300 аутентичными рукописными решениями студентов для оценки возможностей мультимодальных больших языковых моделей в распознавании сложных STEM-задач. Исследование выявило серьёзные ограничения современных MLLM в точной интерпретации переплетённых математических формул, диаграмм и текстового рассуждения в рукописном формате.
